title: Pets: reading realistic fiction; A New Pet
description: 3rd grade reading comprehension practice using the story A New Pet
topic_path: Pets: reading realistic fiction; A New Pet

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the story, then answer the practice question.

---

##A New Pet##

![Diego is lying back on a crescent moon in the night sky, asleep. A thought bubble shows him dreaming of a grey dog with floppy ears.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c4fcaac6bde993440a04120f32af87c7.png)
*Picture 1*

1. For as long as Diego could remember he wanted a dog. Hardly a week would go by without him pestering his grandmother about it. And every time, her answer was the same. “No, Diego we can’t get a dog! We live in a small two-bedroom apartment in the city. We’re already bursting at the seams. Not to mention taking a dog for a walk is difficult. We have the disadvantage of living a full six blocks from the nearest park. How is a dog supposed to get exercise every day? Plus, we would have to find time to groom a dog.”
 
2. Diego’s response was always the same. “I promise to walk the dog every day, Abuela. You wouldn’t have to do anything!”
 
3. But Diego’s grandmother never gave in. One day after school, Diego’s grandmother told Diego they were going out. “Where are we going, Abuela?” Diego asked. 
 
4. “Just come with me,” replied his grandmother.
 
5. *Where on earth are we going?* Diego wondered as they walked. *It’s a school night.*

![Diego and his grandmother walking outside. Diego looks happy and curious; his grandmother looks a little serious.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d49b089ec5e68a8719df01ff111dcfab.png)
 *Picture 2*

6. To Diego’s shock, his grandmother led him to the animal shelter. As they entered, Diego’s grandmother turned around and said, “Diego, you’ve worked so hard at school and I am very proud. To show you how responsible I think you are, I am going to let you get a pet. But it can’t be a dog. We simply don’t have the room for a dog. And we don’t have a yard for a dog to play in. You can pick either a fish or a hamster.”
 
7. Diego felt disappointed. A fish or a hamster was nothing like a dog! Fish and hamsters weren’t loyal and sweet. They didn’t show affection to their owners. Diego didn’t think either of them would be a good companion. *But maybe if I show Abuela how responsible I am with a pet, she’ll eventually let me get a dog,* he thought. 
 
8. Diego considered both the fish and the hamster. When he took the hamster from the cage, he realized that, in fact, a hamster was similar to a dog after all. The hamster showed Diego affection by nuzzling his hand. The hamster’s soft hair could be groomed. But more importantly, that little hamster provided comfort to Diego and would be a nice companion. “I’ll call you Phil,” Diego declared to his new friend, as he gently petted Phil’s soft spotted fur. 

![Diego holding Phil the hamster. Diego looks very happy.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2b7d13833c0abe9c17076190d70f9365.png)
*Picture 3* 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

---

##Practice question##

**What detail from the text best shows Diego’s determination?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: “For as long as Diego could remember he wanted a dog. Hardly a week would go by without him pestering his grandmother about it.”
“To Diego’s shock, his grandmother led him to the animal shelter.”
‘“I promise to walk the dog every day, Abuela. You wouldn’t have to do anything!”’
“Diego felt disappointed. A fish or a hamster was nothing like a dog! Fish and hamsters weren’t loyal and sweet.”

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the story, then answer the practice question.

3. But Diego’s grandmother never gave in. One day after school, Diego’s grandmother told Diego they were going out. “Where are we going, Abuela?” Diego asked.

##Practice question##


**Why does Diego’s Grandmother let him get a pet?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: because she is proud of him for working so hard
because she gave in to his constant asking
because she always wanted to own a pet
because she thinks that he needs to learn a lesson

##Practice question##


**How do Diego’s feelings change when he learns that his grandmother is going to let him get a pet?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: At first, Diego feels disappointed that he’s not getting a dog, but then he feels happy to have a hamster.
At first, Diego feels excited about getting a hamster, but then he feels worried about being able to take care of it.
At first, Diego feels upset that his grandmother is taking him out on a school night, but then he feels excited about getting a hamster
At first, Diego is happy he’ll be able to give an animal a home, but then he feels mad that he has to get a hamster.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the story, then answer the practice question.

![Diego holding Phil the hamster. Diego looks very happy.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2b7d13833c0abe9c17076190d70f9365.png)
*Picture 3* 

[[☃ explanation 3]]

choices.content: that Diego has not given up his dream of getting a dog
that Diego is happy with Phil and doesn’t care about getting a dog anymore
that Diego hopes his grandmother will let him get a dog tomorrow
that Diego understands he will never be allowed to get a dog

##A New Pet##

![Diego is lying back on a crescent moon in the night sky, asleep. A thought bubble shows him dreaming of a grey dog with floppy ears.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c4fcaac6bde993440a04120f32af87c7.png)
*Picture 1*

1. For as long as Diego could remember he wanted a dog. Hardly a week would go by without him pestering his grandmother about it. And every time, her answer was the same. “No, Diego we can’t get a dog! We live in a small two-bedroom apartment in the city. We’re already bursting at the seams. Not to mention taking a dog for a walk is difficult. We have the disadvantage of living a full six blocks from the nearest park. How is a dog supposed to get exercise every day? Plus, we would have to find time to groom a dog.”
 
2. Diego’s response was always the same. “I promise to walk the dog every day, Abuela. You wouldn’t have to do anything!”
 
3. But Diego’s grandmother never gave in. One day after school, Diego’s grandmother told Diego they were going out. “Where are we going, Abuela?” Diego asked. 
 
4. “Just come with me,” replied his grandmother.
 
5. *Where on earth are we going?* Diego wondered as they walked. *It’s a school night.*

![Diego and his grandmother walking outside. Diego looks happy and curious; his grandmother looks a little serious.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d49b089ec5e68a8719df01ff111dcfab.png)
 *Picture 2*

6. To Diego’s shock, his grandmother led him to the animal shelter. As they entered, Diego’s grandmother turned around and said, “Diego, you’ve worked so hard at school and I am very proud. To show you how responsible I think you are, I am going to let you get a pet. But it can’t be a dog. We simply don’t have the room for a dog. And we don’t have a yard for a dog to play in. You can pick either a fish or a hamster.”
 
7. Diego felt disappointed. A fish or a hamster was nothing like a dog! Fish and hamsters weren’t loyal and sweet. They didn’t show affection to their owners. Diego didn’t think either of them would be a good companion. *But maybe if I show Abuela how responsible I am with a pet, she’ll eventually let me get a dog,* he thought. 
 
8. Diego considered both the fish and the hamster. When he took the hamster from the cage, he realized that, in fact, a hamster was similar to a dog after all. The hamster showed Diego affection by nuzzling his hand. The hamster’s soft hair could be groomed. But more importantly, that little hamster provided comfort to Diego and would be a nice companion. “I’ll call you Phil,” Diego declared to his new friend, as he gently petted Phil’s soft spotted fur. 

![Diego holding Phil the hamster. Diego looks very happy.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2b7d13833c0abe9c17076190d70f9365.png)
*Picture 3* 

[[☃ explanation 3]]
